executiveSummary: Our MBA in Real Estate program features a Spiral Learning Model, offering two distinct yet complementary tracks: the Academic Curriculum (SPPU Track) covering core management foundations, and the Industrial Curriculum (Lotlite Track) focusing on CRM, sales, and modern property service delivery.

spiralLearning.title: SPIRAL LEARNING MODEL
spiralLearning.paragraph: Instead of a traditional linear curriculum where subjects are taught in isolation, our program utilizes a Continuous Progressive Model. Students are introduced to all core functional domains right from Semester I. As they progress through the 24-month program, they revisit these exact same domains each semester with increasing complexity, moving from foundational concepts to industry-level execution.

points.title: Immediate Holistic Understanding
points.description: Students grasp how different facets of Real Estate and Management interconnect from Day 1.

points.title: Reinforced Learning
points.description: Rehearsing and building upon the same subjects over four semesters drastically improves retention and practical application.

points.title: Job Readiness from the Start
points.description: Training and core execution are woven into every stage, developing a continuous portfolio of work.

valuePropositions.title: Comprehensive Management Foundation
valuePropositions.description: Develop strong business acumen with core subjects like Economics, Finance, and Marketing.

valuePropositions.title: Specialized Real Estate Focus
valuePropositions.description: Gain deep insights into Real Estate Economics, Property Management, and Urban Infrastructure.

valuePropositions.title: Practical Industry Exposure
valuePropositions.description: Apply theoretical knowledge through a Summer Internship Project and a final Capstone Project.

sppu.focus: Generic Core (GC): Managerial Economics, Organizational Behaviour, Financial Accounting, Business Research Methods. Subject Core (SC): Marketing Management, Design Thinking.

modules.title: Foundational Management Principles
modules.description: Core concepts of organizational behavior and management tailored for modern property enterprises.

modules.title: Real Estate Marketing Management
modules.description: Strategic frameworks for positioning, branding, and promoting residential and commercial real estate assets.

modules.title: Fundamentals of CRM
modules.description: Introduction to building and sustaining long-term loyalty with property buyers, tenants, and institutional investors.

modules.title: Property Buyer & Consumer Behavior
modules.description: Understanding the psychological and economic drivers behind real estate investments and home-buying decisions.

sppu.focus: Generic Core (GC): Financial Management, Human Resource Management, Operations & Supply Chain Management. Subject Core (SC): Legal Aspects of Business. Skill Lab: Desk Research, Field Projects, Selling & Negotiation Skills.

modules.title: Advanced CRM Strategies
modules.description: A deep dive into client lifecycle management, personalized engagement, and retention tactics in a competitive housing market.

modules.title: Sales & Channel Management
modules.description: Effectively managing brokerage networks, channel partners, and direct property sales pipelines.

modules.title: Property Services Marketing
modules.description: Promoting intangible real estate services, including facility management, leasing, and advisory.

modules.title: CRM in the Real Estate Service Sector
modules.description: Tailoring client relationship frameworks specifically for property management and urban infrastructure services.

sppu.focus: Project Management: Planning, scheduling, and executing complex projects. Real Estate Economics & Finance: Real estate valuation, funding, and appraisal methods. Legal Aspects in Real Estate: Regulatory frameworks, contract management, and land laws. Summer Internship Project (SIP): Mandatory industry training (8-10 weeks).

modules.title: Strategic Business Communication
modules.description: Mastering stakeholder communication, negotiation, and high-stakes client pitching in real estate transactions.

modules.title: B2B Markets & Commercial CRM
modules.description: Managing complex relationships with corporate tenants, institutional investors, and B2B commercial leasing partners.

modules.title: Real Estate Market Research
modules.description: Data-driven approaches to analyzing property trends, demographic shifts, and evolving client needs.

modules.title: E-Business & PropTech Ecosystems
modules.description: Leveraging digital platforms, virtual tours, and tech-enabled sales processes to enhance the buyer journey.

sppu.focus: Sustainable Real Estate & Urban Infrastructure: Green building norms and smart city development principles. Facilities & Property Management: Operation, maintenance, and optimization of built assets. Dissertation / Capstone Project: A research-based or practical implementation project on a real-world real estate problem.

modules.title: Consumer Protection & Real Estate Law
modules.description: Navigating legal frameworks, consumer rights, and RERA (Real Estate Regulatory Authority) guidelines to ensure ethical and transparent client dealings.

modules.title: Strategic Real Estate Marketing
modules.description: High-level branding and go-to-market strategies for mega-projects, smart cities, and luxury developments.

modules.title: Research Methodology
modules.description: Frameworks for conducting deep dives into urban infrastructure data and buyer demographics.

modules.title: Final Capstone Project
modules.description: A hands-on, practical implementation or research project focused on solving a real-world CRM challenge within the real estate industry.
